title: Ethiopia’s Trade Union Movement Is Growing Stronger
summary: When the General Assembly of the Confederation of Ethiopian Trade Unions (CETU) met in December 2025, it confirmed that the membership of its affiliated unions had, for the first time in Ethiopian history, surpassed one million workers, organized in 2,653 basic enterprise unions. This denotes remarkable growth for a trade union movement whose membership figures […]
reason: A concrete example of workers building organizational infrastructure that routes labor power through collective institutions rather than market or state intermediaries, at genuine scale.
angle: Use the routing architecture tool: ask what circuits of solidarity and resource-sharing this million-member threshold makes possible that didn't exist before, and what enclosure pressures it will face as it becomes legible to capital.

title: Journalist Sues FAA Over Drone No Fly Zone Designed to Prevent Filming ICE
summary: A Minnesota journalist is challenging a 3,000 foot restriction on flying near DHS assets on First Amendment grounds.
reason: The FAA no-fly zone is a concrete example of airspace being used as a routing architecture to prevent documentation of state violence — enclosure of the visual commons.
angle: Use the routing architecture tool: the no-fly zone doesn't censor content directly, it forecloses the infrastructure of witness — who can see what, from where, determines what enters public record at all.

title: Artemis II Astronauts Have ‘Two Microsoft Outlooks’ and Neither Work

title: A Secure Chat App’s Encryption Is So Bad It Is ‘Meaningless’
summary: TeleGuard is an app downloaded more a million times that markets itself as a secure way to chat. The app uploads users’ private keys to the company’s server, and makes decryption of messages trivial.
reason: TeleGuard's fake privacy architecture shows simulation in operation: it mimics the routing properties of genuinely decentralized secure communication while actually centralizing all control, making it a concrete example of aesthetic capture of the commons-layer infrastructure that end-to-end encryption represents.
angle: Use the simulation tool — TeleGuard doesn't just fail at security, it actively replaces the real thing (distributed key control as a routing-level change) with a managed representation of security, capturing user trust while routing all data through a central chokepoint.

title: State Bill Would Stop Highway Expansions Near Vulnerable New Yorkers
summary: Assembly Member Emerita Torres's Stop Highway Community Harm Act would ban the state from expanding highways within 200 feet of public housing or in ZIP codes with the highest asthma-related emergency room visits in the state.
reason: The bill directly contests the routing architecture of infrastructure investment that has historically channeled highway expansion through low-income communities of color, making visible the enclosure of common health and space.
angle: Use the routing architecture tool to show how highway placement has never been neutral—it routes pollution, noise, and displacement through specific bodies while routing mobility benefits elsewhere, and this bill proposes a structural rather than representational intervention.

title: Exclusive: Meta has discussed ending funding to the Oversight Board
summary: Shifting priorities and budget pressures could bring an end to the company’s experiment in independent governance, sources say
reason: Meta potentially dismantling its own constructed alibi for content moderation legitimacy makes the alibi structure visible in real time.
angle: The Oversight Board was always a routing mechanism disguised as a governance mechanism — it routed legitimacy claims to Meta without routing actual decision-making power away from it, and its defunding reveals that alibi at the moment Meta no longer needs it.

title: Stop brunch! How a rustic Catalan meal is taking the fight to bland food and overtourism | Abbas Asaria
summary: As Barcelona groans under a surfeit of generic cafes, a grassroots movement is reviving the traditional ‘fork breakfast’. Anyone for pigs’ trotters? There are many worse ways to start your day than with eggs royale. The contrast in textures between a soft poached egg and a coarse, toasted English muffin is a thing of beauty, and the combination of smoked salmon and a lemony hollandaise sauce ties it together perfectly. The term “brunch” was coined in an essay in Hunter’s Weekly in 1895, and while you’re unlikely to find too many fans in foodie circles , or among those who have to work the shift (“nothing demoralises an aspiring Escoffier faster”, wrote Anthony Bourdain ), they aren’t lacking in number. It clearly has its place. The problem is the place it currently occupies: in our gentrif
